title: sherardia arvensis subsp. maritima
intro: Sherardia arvensis subsp. maritima, also known as sea purslane, is a succulent plant species in the family Aizoaceae. It is a perennial herb native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa, and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of sea purslane include its ability to support immune function, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant properties, making it a potential treatment for various health conditions such as arthritis, wounds, and skin irritations. Sea purslane can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and salves, which can be made by drying the leaves and stems, then infusing them in hot water or oil to release their medicinal compounds.
